meta.title: Input
meta.description: Text entry components — Text Input, Text Input with Icon, and Search Input — plus numerical, table, list, and description variants.

hero.title: Input
hero.desc: Input lets users enter and edit text. The family includes Text Input, Text Input with Icon, and Search Input — used to query and retrieve specific values.

dropdownType.title: Dropdown Type
dropdownType.desc: Dropdowns surface Menu Items used to filter or sort page content. Variants split by Dropdown Button type — Primary (Label), Secondary (Avatar & Badge), and Tertiary (Non-Outlined).

rows.label: Extra Composition
rows.primary: Leading Icon, Clear All Icon Button

rows.label: Characteristics
rows.primary: The most common type — composed of a text-only Label Dropdown or a Label with Icon Dropdown.
rows.secondary: Outline hidden until Focused — at which point the field falls back to a Primary-style Text (with Icon) Input. Used inside tables and lists.
rows.extra: Forms outside Primary and Secondary — primarily Text Area, Comment Text Input, and Numerical Text Input.

components.title: Components
components.desc: Input splits into Primary and Secondary by style, intent, and field shape. Within each, sub-types — Text (with Icon) Input and Search Input — apply per use case.

textWithIcon.title: Text (with Icon) Input
textWithIcon.desc: The most common type — composed of a text-only Label Input or a Label with Icon Input.

secondary.title: Secondary Type
secondary.desc: Outline hidden by default; when Focused, the input falls back to a Primary-style Text (with Icon) form. Two variants: Table Input and List Input. Table Input keeps the area's padding; List Input fills the entire cell (padding = 0).

extra.title: Extra Type
extra.desc: Forms outside Primary and Secondary — Description Text Area (height flexes with content up to a max), Comment Text Input (fixed height), and Numerical Text Input (numeric data only).

state.title: State
state.desc: Input has 4 states — Disabled, Default, Hover, Focused.

size.title: Size
size.desc: Sizes use height — Large, Medium, Small. Width stays flexible.

minWidth.title: Minimum Width
minWidth.text: Width flexes with the surrounding area. Minimum width is 160px.

overflow.title: Text Overflow
overflow.text: Overflowing text renders one of two ways — truncate with "…" at a fixed height, or wrap to the next line beyond the given width. Choose per feature intent.

search.title: Search
search.desc: Search Input lets users query for specific information. Unlike other Input components, when Fill = On, the Cancel (Clear All) Icon Button surfaces.

withLabel.title: Text Input with Field Label
withLabel.desc: For in-page item search — uses the Container outline form.

withoutLabel.title: Text Input without Field Label
withoutLabel.desc: For searching inside another component — uses the underline form.

textIconSection.title: Text (with Icon) Input
textIconSection.desc:
Surfaces a field for text entry. Typically paired with a Field Label.
(e.g. Status / Comment text in the OSD Viewer panel, History panel text entry, Login form input.)

tableList.title: Table / List Input
tableList.desc: Edit values inline inside a Table or List. On Hover and Focused, Table / List Inputs match the Primary Type Input behavior.

numerical.title: Numerical Input
numerical.desc: Accepts numeric values only. Used without a Label — the Unit sits to the right with a small gap.

withUnit.title: Numerical Input with unit
withUnit.desc: Paired with a unit — often combined with other controls (checkbox, slider, etc.).

state.title: State of Numerical Input
state.desc: Four states — Disabled, Default, Hover, Focused — each with Filled or Unfilled. Unfilled placeholders carry one of three values: "0", "min", "max".

description.title: Description / Comment
description.desc:
Description and Comment patterns surface text for reading or capture text from users. Read-focused Text Area vs. entry-focused Text Input — the container height flexes or stays fixed by context.

The same text-based component delivers a consistent input experience tailored to each surface's purpose and flow.

descArea.title: Description Text Area
descArea.desc: Conveys descriptive text for easy reading. Used for guidance copy, status descriptions, supplementary info — read-focused content. Expands across one line or multiple lines based on content.

descInput.title: Description Text Input
descInput.desc: For user-entered descriptions or detail text. In fixed-height panels like the History Panel, input runs within the panel's preserved structure.

commentArea.title: Comment Text Area
commentArea.desc: For free-length opinions or comments. The container height expands with the entered text — supports natural multi-line capture.

commentInput.title: Commnet Text Input
commentInput.desc: For short comments or quick replies. Fixed input height — efficient, concise capture in a constrained area.

usage.title: Usage & Placement
usage.desc: Input most commonly appears in form layouts, often paired with Dropdown.

label.title: Label
label.text: Forms with a Dropdown surface a Label for the field. Use Subtitle 3 (Inter, Medium, 14px), with Required, Error, and Information variants as needed. Label + Dropdown / Input combinations are often used together. Use 8px between Label and Dropdown, 24px between horizontally arranged elements, and 32px between vertically arranged. In vertical-only layouts, use 16px between elements.

sync.title: Label: Sync
sync.text: A Sync Checkbox to the right of the Label auto-syncs the data entered into the field.

information.title: Label: Information
information.text: Surfaces supplementary information about the input value to the user.

required.title: Label: Required
required.text: Marks the field as required. Place 4px after the Label and use "*". Optional fields use no marker — just the Label. Use Body 2 (Inter, Regular, 14px) for "*", at the same text size as the Label.

error.title: Label: Error(Alert)
error.text: Surfaces an alert about the input value. Typically triggered after an action (e.g., Confirm Button). The Dropdown Button outline color and a Text Alert both surface. Error state outline color: Red 500. Use the Medium-size Alert.

labelSize.title: Label Size
labelSize.text: Form-resident Inputs with Labels use the Large or Medium Primary Type Text Input. Label typography differs per size.

unit.title: Unit
unit.text: When the Input value is numeric, surface its unit information in the Label. Format as Label (Unit) using parentheses. Numerical Inputs aren't paired with a Label — when a unit is needed, place it 4px outside the right edge of the field.
